one_line_takeaway: Rewriting harmful jailbreak prompts into deliberately obscure, archaic language exploits the OOD fragility of LLM safety alignment, giving a practical black-box attack that beats prior baselines on hard models and survives perplexity filtering (though partly blunted by paraphrasing).
key_finding: ObscurePrompt raises ASR over all baselines on the harder targets: Llama2-7b 0.6664 (vs AutoDAN-HGA 0.6080), Llama2-70b 0.5082 (vs best baseline DeepInception 0.1228, roughly a 38-point gain), ChatGPT 0.8931 (vs DeepInception 0.7024), Llama3-8b 0.3105 and Llama3-70b 0.2552 (best in their columns), and GPT-4 0.2697 (vs PAIR 0.2528). On Vicuna-7b it scores 0.9373, just below white-box GCG's 0.9710. Larger models within a family are harder to jailbreak (a safety 'scaling law': Llama2-70b < Llama2-7b in ASR, Llama3-70b < Llama3-8b), ASR rises with more integrated prompts, and combining ALL attack types is often not optimal (ChatGPT and GPT-4 peak using only 'Start With'). Against defenses: paraphrasing cuts ASR substantially (e.g., Llama2-70b 0.7525 -> 0.2442) but leaves meaningful residual risk (GPT-4 17.74%, ChatGPT 52.54%), and PPL filtering is ineffective because obscure prompts' perplexity overlaps benign harmless queries, so filtering them would also block legitimate traffic.
core_contribution: The paper introduces ObscurePrompt, a training-free, black-box jailbreak method for aligned LLMs (the title uses the generic phrasing 'Jailbreaking LLMs Through Alignment Vulnerabilities in Out-of-Distribution Settings', but the authors' own named contribution is ObscurePrompt). The central idea is that LLM safety alignment is fragile on out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs, so deliberately rewriting a harmful jailbreak prompt into deliberately 'obscure' (archaic, verbose, euphemistic) phrasing pushes the prompt's internal representation away from the model's learned 'harmful' concept region and across the model's ethical decision boundary. The pipeline has three stages: (1) Prompt Seed Curation - build a base prompt combining established jailbreak tricks (Forget Restraints, Direct Answer, Avoid Sorry, Start With Specified Sentences); (2) Obscure-Guided Transformation - use GPT-4 to rewrite the seed prompt to be more obscure (literal instruction 'Please make the following text more obscure'); (3) Attack Integration - repeat n times to build a set of n obscure prompts, and if any one succeeds the attack counts as successful. The paper also contributes a motivating empirical observation (PCA of Llama2-7b top-layer embeddings) showing obscure full prompts sit farthest from the harmful-query cluster.
threat_model: Black-box adversary with only query access to a deployed aligned LLM (no weights, no gradients, no logits needed). The attacker can call a separate powerful LLM (GPT-4) as an offline prompt-obscuring tool. Goal is to elicit harmful/prohibited content (advbench-style: weapons, hacking, disinformation, self-harm). This is explicitly positioned as more practical than prior white-box attacks (GCG, AutoDAN) that require internal access and than fixed-template attacks that require manual crafting. Defender is assumed to possibly deploy input paraphrasing or perplexity (PPL) filtering.
limitations: (1) Success is judged by keyword/refusal matching, which the authors adopt because obscure responses confound an LLM-judge; this can over- or under-count genuinely harmful output. (2) The attack depends on an external strong LLM (GPT-4) to perform the obscuring, so its power is coupled to that model's capability and willingness to rewrite harmful text. (3) The 'success if any of n prompts works' criterion inflates ASR relative to single-shot attacks and grows with n. (4) Absolute ASR on the strongest proprietary model (GPT-4, 0.2697) and on Llama3 models remains modest, and the paper's own paraphrase-defense results show the method is partially mitigable. (5) The submitted PDF is an unfinished ACM template (placeholder CCS/keywords like 'Do Not Use This Code' and dummy 2018 conference metadata), and semantic-shift similarity for obscure queries (0.619) is slightly below plain paraphrase (0.640), indicating some meaning drift.

datasets_used: advbench (520 harmful queries; 100 sampled for the PCA motivation study)
OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (used to measure semantic similarity of original vs obscure queries)
GPT-2 (used to compute perplexity distributions for the PPL-filtering analysis)
quantitative_results: ObscurePrompt ASR - Vicuna-7b 0.9373, Llama2-7b 0.6664, Llama2-70b 0.5082, Llama3-8b 0.3105, Llama3-70b 0.2552, ChatGPT 0.8931, GPT-4 0.2697 (Table 2, integrated prompt number 5)
Llama2-70b: ObscurePrompt 0.5082 vs best baseline DeepInception 0.1228 (~38-point improvement)
Llama2-7b: ObscurePrompt 0.6664 vs AutoDAN-HGA 0.6080, AutoDAN-GA 0.5620, GCG 0.4540, DeepInception 0.3051, PAIR 0.0821
Paraphrase defense residual ASR: Llama2-70b drops 0.7525 -> 0.2442; GPT-4 residual 17.74%; ChatGPT residual 52.54%
Vicuna-7b prompt-sensitivity variance and std = 0 because it hit 100% ASR on all prompts
Semantic similarity original-vs-obscure 0.619 vs original-vs-paraphrase 0.640; human query-consistency ~0.967 average
baselines_compared: AutoDAN (white-box; GA and HGA hierarchical genetic variants)
